title: deserialize_file_name: no maximum filename length cap
description: messages.rs:218 deserialize_file_name rejects path separators, null bytes, and the special names '..' and '.' but imposes no length limit. A hostile peer can send a FileHeader with a 10,000-character filename that passes all checks, then the CLI writes it to disk with that name (or passes it to safe_name() which preserves length). Most filesystems cap at 255 bytes; exceeding this causes a write error rather than a clean rejection. Fix: add a length check (e.g., >255 bytes is rejected) before the path-separator check.
